text.title: gitlab-boards Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gitlab-boards. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use one canonical workflow board per team; additional boards are filtered views, not parallel state machines.
rule.rationale: Parallel state machines create conflicting states — an issue can appear "done" on one board and "in-progress" on another. One board, many views.

rule.statement: Encode all state machines as scoped labels using the namespace::value syntax; keep active label count at or below 20.
rule.rationale: Scoped labels enforce mutual exclusivity — GitLab allows only one label per namespace per issue, preventing conflicting states (e.g., two workflow:: labels).

rule.statement: Set WIP limits per column (Premium) and enforce them at card entry — block rather than warn.
rule.rationale: WIP limits are the primary mechanism to surface bottlenecks. Warning-only enforcement is ignored under delivery pressure.

rule.statement: Configure iteration cadence at the group level so all teams share the same sprint boundary.
rule.rationale: Group-level cadence enables cross-team planning in iteration reports; misaligned boundaries make portfolio-level rollups meaningless.

rule.statement: Auto-close via "Closes #N" only on default-branch merges; for feature-branch flows use deploy-job notifications.
rule.rationale: Auto-close depends on the default branch target; merges to feature branches will not close issues, leaving board state stale.

rule.statement: Use weight consistently: 0 = unestimated, 1-2 = trivial, 3-5 = standard, 8-13 = large (must break down), above 13 = epic candidate.
rule.rationale: Mixing weight semantics (story points vs. hours vs. priority) destroys iteration capacity planning.

rule.statement: Encode the workflow as scoped labels in a YAML file (.gitlab/labels.yaml) applied via a CI job — never configure labels manually at scale.
rule.rationale: Manual label configuration drifts across projects in a group; idempotent CI application (via gitlabform) keeps all projects consistent.

rule.statement: Keep label count at or below 20 active labels; archive deprecated labels rather than reusing or deleting.
rule.rationale: Reusing deprecated labels corrupts history — old issues inherit the new meaning. Archiving preserves the audit trail.

rule.statement: Tie every MR to an issue; every closed issue must reference an MR (except docs-only).
rule.rationale: Traceability from requirement to code change is the primary compliance and debugging artifact in regulated environments.

rule.statement: Use Roadmaps + Epics for portfolio (Premium+); keep epics at 3 levels maximum.
rule.rationale: Deep nesting (4+ levels) makes roadmap rendering unreadable and cross-team planning impossible without a dedicated tool.
